edges.description: Additional somatic DICER1 alteration creates lesion-level dysfunction.
RNase IIIb dysfunction impairs normal 5p miRNA maturation.
Loss of epithelial miRNA control permits excess FGF9 signaling in early PPB.
Excess epithelial FGF9 stimulates neighboring mesenchymal expansion.
Mesenchymal hyperplasia remodels the developing lung into cystic PPB.
A subset of cystic lesions progresses to type II or III PPB.
TP53 loss accompanies the transition to aggressive type II/III disease.
RAS pathway mutations are additional contributors to progression from cysts to tumors.

nodes.description: DICER1-disrupted PPB retains abnormal 5p miRNA hairpin-loop sequence and reduces normal miRNA-mediated post-transcriptional repression.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: 5p-Derived microRNA (miRNA) transcripts retained abnormal precursor miRNA loop sequences normally removed by DICER1.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: In addition to frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and mutations of NRAS or BRAF in some cases, each case had compound disruption of DICER1: a germline (12 cases) or somatic (3 cases) loss-of-function variant plus a somatic missense mutation in the RNase IIIb domain.

nodes.description: Progressive PPB typically combines a germline or somatic DICER1 loss-of-function allele with a somatic RNase IIIb hotspot mutation, fitting a specialized two-hit tumor suppressor model.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: In addition to frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and mutations of NRAS or BRAF in some cases, each case had compound disruption of DICER1: a germline (12 cases) or somatic (3 cases) loss-of-function variant plus a somatic missense mutation in the RNase IIIb domain.

meta.association: Germline Loss-of-Function with Somatic RNase IIIb Second Hit

evidence.reference_title: DICER1 mutations in familial pleuropulmonary blastoma.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that 11 multiplex PPB families harbor heterozygous germline mutations in DICER1, a gene encoding an endoribonuclease critical to the generation of small noncoding regulatory RNAs.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: In addition to frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and mutations of NRAS or BRAF in some cases, each case had compound disruption of DICER1: a germline (12 cases) or somatic (3 cases) loss-of-function variant plus a somatic missense mutation in the RNase IIIb domain.

nodes.description: Type I PPB shows excess epithelial FGF9 expression, linking epithelial DICER1-associated miRNA dysregulation to paracrine growth signaling.

biological_processes: fibroblast growth factor receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast Growth Factor 9 Regulation by MicroRNAs Controls Lung Development and Links DICER1 Loss to the Pathogenesis of Pleuropulmonary Blastoma.
evidence.snippet: FGF9 is overexpressed in lung epithelium in the initial multicystic stage of Type I PPB

nodes.description: Heterozygous germline DICER1 loss-of-function variants create the inherited predisposition context for many PPB cases and place the disease within the DICER1 tumor predisposition syndrome spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: DICER1 mutations in familial pleuropulmonary blastoma.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that 11 multiplex PPB families harbor heterozygous germline mutations in DICER1, a gene encoding an endoribonuclease critical to the generation of small noncoding regulatory RNAs.

evidence.reference_title: Pleuropulmonary blastoma: a report on 350 central pathology-confirmed pleuropulmonary blastoma cases by the International Pleuropulmonary Blastoma Registry.
evidence.snippet: A germline mutation in DICER1 is the genetic cause in the majority of PPB cases.

nodes.description: The earliest PPB lesion is a delicate multilocular cyst with subepithelial primitive mesenchymal cells; some lesions regress to type Ir, whereas others progress toward solid sarcoma.

evidence.reference_title: Type I pleuropulmonary blastoma: pathology and biology study of 51 cases from the international pleuropulmonary blastoma registry.
evidence.snippet: Type I PPB is a delicate multilocular cyst with variable numbers of primitive mesenchymal cells beneath a benign epithelial surface.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: In addition to frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and mutations of NRAS or BRAF in some cases, each case had compound disruption of DICER1: a germline (12 cases) or somatic (3 cases) loss-of-function variant plus a somatic missense mutation in the RNase IIIb domain.

nodes.description: Increased epithelial FGF9 drives non-cell-autonomous expansion of pulmonary mesenchyme and builds the multicystic type I PPB architecture.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast Growth Factor 9 Regulation by MicroRNAs Controls Lung Development and Links DICER1 Loss to the Pathogenesis of Pleuropulmonary Blastoma.
evidence.snippet: increased Fgf9 expression results in pulmonary mesenchymal hyperplasia and a multicystic architecture that is histologically and molecularly indistinguishable from Type I PPB.

nodes.description: Somatic NRAS or BRAF mutations occur in a subset of PPB and add oncogenic signaling support to the progressive sarcoma phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: In addition to frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and mutations of NRAS or BRAF in some cases, each case had compound disruption of DICER1: a germline (12 cases) or somatic (3 cases) loss-of-function variant plus a somatic missense mutation in the RNase IIIb domain.

nodes.description: PPB can remain cystic or evolve into type II/III high-grade sarcoma with markedly worse outcomes and metastatic risk.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: Pleuropulmonary blastoma is a rare childhood malignancy of lung mesenchymal cells that can remain dormant as epithelial cysts or progress to high-grade sarcoma.

evidence.reference_title: Pleuropulmonary blastoma: a report on 350 central pathology-confirmed pleuropulmonary blastoma cases by the International Pleuropulmonary Blastoma Registry.
evidence.snippet: The 5-year overall survival (OS) rate for type I/Ir patients was 91%; all deaths in this group were due to progression to type II or III.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing of pleuropulmonary blastoma reveals frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and two hits in DICER1 resulting in retention of 5p-derived miRNA hairpin loop sequences.
evidence.snippet: In addition to frequent biallelic loss of TP53 and mutations of NRAS or BRAF in some cases, each case had compound disruption of DICER1: a germline (12 cases) or somatic (3 cases) loss-of-function variant plus a somatic missense mutation in the RNase IIIb domain.

nodes.description: Frequent biallelic TP53 loss is a recurrent cooperating event in progressive PPB and supports evolution toward high-grade solid sarcoma.
